name: Ledge Climb
description: Ledge Climb is a precision movement mechanic used to negotiate platforms, walls, gaps, and hazards.
design_notes: Ledge Climb should be designed around a clear player promise, a readable trigger, and a predictable result. For platforming mechanics, the important design work is deciding when the mechanic is allowed, how much commitment it creates, what feedback confirms success, and what counter-pressure keeps it from solving every situation. Strong tuning makes the mechanic feel intentional even when it is used repeatedly or combined with adjacent systems.
design_purpose: Give the player a readable way to use ledge climb as part of the core loop.
Create meaningful choices around timing, cost, risk, positioning, or long-term planning.
Connect ledge climb to adjacent systems so it supports the larger game design instead of acting as an isolated feature.
player_fantasy: The player feels precise, agile, and responsible for overcoming spatial challenges.

parameters.name: activation_window
parameters.description: Timing window in which the platforming action can start.

parameters.name: vertical_velocity
parameters.description: Initial or sustained vertical movement contribution.

parameters.name: control_lockout
parameters.description: Short period where player input is limited to preserve readability.

edge_cases: Ledge Climb is triggered during a scene transition, pause, respawn, or save/load boundary.
Ledge Climb overlaps with another high-priority state such as damage, stun, dialogue, menu focus, or cutscene control.
Ledge Climb runs at extreme frame rates, high latency, very large coordinates, or after long play sessions.
common_bugs: Ledge Climb state does not reset correctly after interruption or reload.
Feedback for Ledge Climb plays even when the mechanic fails or is rejected.
Ledge Climb produces different results between frame rates, platforms, or network clients.
balancing_notes: Make Ledge Climb strong enough to justify attention but limited enough that other mechanics keep their purpose.
Tune costs, cooldowns, ranges, and feedback together; changing only one number can shift the mechanic from optional to dominant.
accessibility_notes: Provide clear visual, audio, or haptic feedback when Ledge Climb becomes available, succeeds, or fails.
Avoid relying on a single sensory cue or extremely narrow timing unless the game offers assists or alternate settings.

implementation_notes.unity: Implement Ledge Climb with engine-agnostic state first, then bind it to MonoBehaviour, CharacterController, Rigidbody, UI Toolkit, or Netcode systems as appropriate.
implementation_notes.godot: Model Ledge Climb as a node or resource-driven state and update deterministic pieces from _physics_process when timing or collision matters.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement Ledge Climb with Actor Components, CharacterMovement, Gameplay Ability System, UMG, or replicated gameplay state depending on the mechanic scope.
implementation_notes.web: Implement Ledge Climb as data-owned state updated from the main loop; keep rendering, input, and simulation boundaries explicit for Canvas, Phaser, Three.js, or custom JavaScript.
